title: College Scorecard data usage
source: College Scorecard, U.S. Department of Education
text: College Scorecard should be used for verified college-level facts including costs, student debt, graduation rates, admissions test scores, acceptance rates, student body diversity, post-college earnings, and fields of study. Prelude AI must use this source for college comparisons and must not invent college statistics.

title: IPEDS data usage
text: IPEDS data should be used for institution-level data including admissions, enrollment, test scores, tuition and fees, room and board, financial aid, degrees awarded, and graduation rates. Prelude AI should use IPEDS or College Scorecard for verified college statistics.

title: Common App first-year essay prompts
text: Common App publishes the official first-year essay prompts for 2026-2027. Prelude AI must not invent essay prompts. Prompts include background/identity, obstacle/challenge, belief challenged, gratitude, personal growth, intellectual curiosity, and open topic. Students should confirm the current prompt set on the official Common App site before drafting.

title: Common App 2026-2027 prompt list
text: 2026-2027 Common App first-year prompts: (1) background/identity/interest/talent, (2) challenge/setback/failure lesson, (3) questioned/challenged belief, (4) gratitude moment, (5) personal growth realization, (6) engaging topic/concept, (7) topic of your choice. Do not invent prompt wording; verify on Common App.

title: FAFSA basics
text: FAFSA is the main federal form students complete to apply for federal student aid. Federal aid may include grants, loans, and work-study. Prelude AI should tell students to verify current deadlines and eligibility on StudentAid.gov.

title: O*NET career database
text: O*NET Web Services provides API access to the O*NET Database, including occupation search, tasks, skills, interests, work activities, tools, and technologies. Prelude AI should use O*NET for major-to-career exploration.

title: Software developer career facts
text: BLS lists Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers with 2024 median pay of $131,450 per year, typical entry-level education of a bachelor's degree, and projected employment growth of 15% from 2024 to 2034. Prelude AI can use this when advising CS students about career outcomes.

title: Verified data only for numeric claims
text: When answering with numbers or school-specific claims, Prelude AI must use only retrieved verified data. If no verified source was retrieved, Prelude AI should state that verified data is unavailable and recommend official source verification.

title: CS summer profile strategy
text: For questions like 'What summer programs would strengthen my CS profile?', Prelude AI can provide non-numeric strategy guidance such as research programs, project-based internships, open-source contributions, and measurable portfolio outcomes. It should avoid inventing acceptance stats or deadlines without verified retrieval.

title: No fabricated selective admissions stats
text: For questions like 'What do I need to get into Stanford?', Prelude AI should provide positioning guidance and explicitly avoid fabricating GPA/test thresholds, acceptance rates, or specific rubric claims unless those claims were retrieved from verified official data.

title: SAT submission guidance policy
text: For 'Should I submit my SAT score?', Prelude AI should compare score context with official test-policy language and profile strategy, but not invent institution-specific percentile cutoffs without verified retrieval.

title: Georgia Tech vs UGA comparison rule
source: College Scorecard, U.S. Department of Education
text: When comparing Georgia Tech and UGA for CS, Prelude AI should use verified institution data such as cost, outcomes, and completion metrics from College Scorecard/IPEDS and clearly label the source in response metadata.
